stem: What is {{n}} rounded to the nearest ten?
solutionStandard: Look at the units digit; round up if it's 5 or more: {{n}} rounds to \({{Math.round(n/10)*10}}\).

stem: What is 6.47 rounded to the nearest tenth?
solutionStandard: The hundredths digit is 7, so round the tenths digit up: \(6.47 \to 6.5\).

stem: Estimate \(297 \times 41\) by rounding each number to the nearest ten, then compute the estimate exactly.
Which of the following is the best estimate of \(\dfrac{5978}{19.8}\)?
\(x = 4.999\) rounded to the nearest whole number.
solutionStandard: \(4.999\) rounds up to \(5\) (the tenths digit is 9). The quantities are equal.

stem: A store's revenue was $48,750 last month. Which of the following is the revenue rounded to the nearest thousand?
solutionStandard: The hundreds digit is 7 (\(\ge5\)), so round the thousands digit up: \(48{,}750 \to 49{,}000\).

stem: A survey estimates a population count by rounding to the nearest hundred. If the rounded value is 3,600, what is the smallest possible actual population?
solutionStandard: The smallest value that rounds up to 3,600 is 3,550 (values from 3,550 up to but not including 3,650 round to 3,600).

stem: Which of the following round to 15.0 when rounded to the nearest tenth? Select all that apply.
solutionStandard: Rounding to the nearest tenth means the hundredths digit determines rounding: \(14.96 \to 15.0\), \(15.04 \to 15.0\). But \(14.94 \to 14.9\) and \(15.06 \to 15.1\).

stem: Which of the following is the best estimate of \(\sqrt{50} \times 11\)?
\(n\) rounds to 200 when rounded to the nearest hundred, and \(n\) is an integer.
solutionStandard: Values from 150 up to and including 249 round to 200 (250 would round up to 300). The greatest possible integer value is 249, equal to Quantity B.
